meta.subject: Spence paternal surname line — Hunter Spence's grandfather David Spence

confidence_tiers.HIGH: Documentary record or multiple concordant primary sources
confidence_tiers.MEDIUM: Documented in reputable genealogy/encyclopedia sources, not independently cross-checked against primary records
confidence_tiers.UNVERIFIED: Family context only, not confirmed in public records

david_spence.name: David Spence
david_spence.birth_year_derivation: ~80 years old as described in Shari Mattingly's interview context, 2025-2026
david_spence.birth_state_reasoning: Inferred from San Antonio context of Shari Mattingly; Scottish-Scots-Irish settlement patterns in Central/South Texas consistent with Spence surname trajectory

parents.note: Unknown — require primary records (Texas birth certificate, 1940-1950 census)

david_spence.privacy_note: Specific identifying details redacted per living-person standards. Research framework documents surname line without exposing private information.

surname_etymology.occupational_meaning: Person in charge of the pantry or food store of a great house, monastery, or castle — a position of middle-rank trust

heraldry.symbolism: Direct reference to the pantry/dispensary role

surname_etymology.relation_to_spencer: Spencer is the extended occupational form meaning 'one who works the spence'; same root

first_documentary_record.location: Charters of Guisburn Priory, Yorkshire, England

source_all_arrivals: Passenger and Immigration Lists Index, Filby & Meyer, Gale Research Co., 1982-1985 (ISBN 0-8103-1795-8)

notable_connection_james_monroe.claim: Patrick Spence migrated to Virginia and was great-grandfather of James Monroe, 5th President of the United States
notable_connection_james_monroe.corroboration: James Monroe's Wikipedia article confirms his father's full name was 'Andrew Spence Monroe' and that Monroe named one of his brothers 'Spence', indicating the Spence name was a significant maternal family name passed down as a given name
notable_connection_james_monroe.connection_type: Monroe's mother's maternal grandfather was reportedly Patrick Spence, a Virginia Spence settler
notable_connection_james_monroe.note_for_hunter: If David Spence's line connects to the same Virginia Spence stock, Hunter would have a distant shared-surname ancestry with the President's maternal line. Cannot be confirmed without primary genealogical records.

migration_corridor_hypothesis.confidence: LOW (general pattern, not confirmed for David's specific line)
migration_corridor_hypothesis.supporting_evidence: Scottish settlement patterns in Texas documented by Texas State Historical Association; Scots arrived singly or in small groups in Texas from 1825 onward; Bexar County (San Antonio) had established Anglo-American settler families by 1840s
migration_corridor_hypothesis.estimated_texas_arrival_of_spence_ancest: 1840s-1880s (third or fourth generation by David's birth ~1945)

notable_spences.name: Thomas Spence
notable_spences.claim_to_fame: Radical land reformer and agrarian activist; author of Spence's Plan (land nationalization); imprisoned in Newgate Gaol 1794 for high treason charge; sentenced 1801 for seditious libel

notable_spences.name: Basil Spence
notable_spences.claim_to_fame: Architect; won competition to rebuild Coventry Cathedral (completed 1962), one of Britain's most significant modern buildings; knighted 1960
notable_spences.education: George Watson's College Edinburgh; Edinburgh College of Art

notable_spences.name: Gerry Spence
notable_spences.claim_to_fame: Trial lawyer famous for never losing a criminal case; defended Karen Silkwood estate, Randy Weaver (Ruby Ridge), acquitted Imelda Marcos; graduated first in University of Wyoming Law class of 1952

notable_spences.name: Brent Spence
notable_spences.claim_to_fame: US Congressman from Kentucky 5th District 1931-1963; Brent Spence Bridge over Ohio River at Cincinnati named for him

notable_spences.name: Catherine Helen Spence
notable_spences.claim_to_fame: Novelist, journalist, women's suffrage leader; first woman to stand for political office in Australia (1897)

notable_spences.name: Skip Spence
notable_spences.claim_to_fame: Rock musician; co-founder Moby Grape; drummer on Jefferson Airplane debut album; solo album 'Oar' (1969)

notable_spences.name: Errol Spence Jr.
notable_spences.claim_to_fame: Professional boxer; former unified WBA/WBC/IBF welterweight champion
notable_spences.note: Different ethnic origin (African-American) from the Scottish-lineage Spence line; included for Texas Spence presence context

notable_spences.name: Patrick Spence (Virginia settler)
notable_spences.claim_to_fame: Migrated to Virginia; documented as great-grandfather of President James Monroe (via Monroe's maternal line)
notable_spences.source: HouseOfNames.com; partially corroborated by Monroe Wikipedia article

recommended_next_research_steps.action: Search FamilySearch.org Texas Birth Certificates 1940-1950 for David Spence
recommended_next_research_steps.notes: Free with account; Texas registered births post-1903

recommended_next_research_steps.action: Search Texas Marriage Index for Spence + Mattingly, 1965-1972
recommended_next_research_steps.notes: Bexar County (San Antonio) most likely; search statewide

recommended_next_research_steps.action: Search 1940 US Census for Spence families in Bexar County and Travis County Texas
recommended_next_research_steps.notes: David's father would appear as a child (born ~1915-1920); look for households with children named David ~1943-1948
Collaborative tree; search for Texas Spence profiles
